items.title: Smart breeding for diversified agricultural systems
items.abstract: Modern plant breeding optimizes varieties for sole cropping, creating trait mismatches that constrain the diversification essential for climate resilience. We propose Smart Systems Breeding, reframing environmental variability as strategic information for precision variety deployment. This approach integrates farmer networks, environmental monitoring and modelling to accelerate breeding for locally adapted intercropping, crop mixtures and agroforestry that supports sustainable agriculture.
items.authors: Ehsan Eyshi Rezaei, Henrik Brinch-Pedersen, Frank Ewert

items.title: Reduced meat and dairy consumption improves health, environmental and most nutritional outcomes without increasing diet costs among Scottish adults
items.abstract: Using microsimulation of Scottish Health Survey adults, this study compares 33 pathways for achieving the UK Climate Change Committee meat and dairy reduction targets. Most pathways improved nutrient intake, health and environmental outcomes without increasing diet costs, with greater benefits from reducing red and processed meat among high consumers and using gram-for-gram substitutions with foods including vegetables, beans, eggs and plant-based alternatives.
items.authors: Joe Kennedy, Michael Clark, Cristina Stewart et al.

items.title: Artificial intelligence for food innovation
items.abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming how new foods are designed, produced and experienced. While AI has the potential to link molecular composition to functional and sensory performance and accelerate food innovation, challenges related to data quality, transparency and governance remain. Addressing these challenges requires interdisciplinary collaboration, open and inclusive data ecosystems and AI systems that augment, rather than replace, human expertise.
items.authors: Bianca Datta, Markus J. Buehler, Yvonne Chow et al.

items.title: Global dietary estimates for conducting health, environmental and economic impact assessments
items.abstract: The Global Dietary Database for Impact Assessments provides information on what people eat across countries and demographic groups by integrating data on food availability, consumption and waste, as well as energy needs. It supports analyses of the impacts of diets on health, environmental and economic outcomes.
items.authors: Marco Springmann
